brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +36.9% since Feb 27 — Brent $97.20/bbl (Apr 14 close) — DOWN 2.2% on diplomatic optimism — US-Iran 'discussing Round 2 Islamabad' — IEA: LARGEST oil supply disruption in history (10.1 mb/d March loss)
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 14 close: $97.20/bbl (-2.2% from Apr 13's $103.00). Oil retreated from Monday's blockade-driven spike as both sides signaled willingness to resume talks. Blockade's first full day saw only 3 commercial vessels transit Hormuz — Rich Starry (Chinese-owned, Malawi-flagged, 250K barrels methanol), Murlikishan (US-sanctioned), Peace Gulf (Panama-flagged heading to UAE) — none to Iranian ports, blockade NOT directly tested. IEA April Oil Market Report confirmed 10.1 mb/d March supply loss to 97 mb/d — largest disruption in history of the global oil market. Hormuz throughput down to 3.8 mb/d (from >20 mb/d pre-war, 80%+ decline). IEA Birol: 'Worse than 1973, 1979, and 2022 combined.' IEA projects steepest quarterly demand decline since COVID; global oil demand to DECLINE 80K b/d in 2026 vs prior +730K growth forecast. Russia's oil revenues nearly doubled to $19B in March. US-Iran 'discussing details for potential second in-person meeting' per CNN; Pakistan PM Sharif + Army Chief Munir mediating Round 2 before Apr 22 ceasefire expires. Energy Secretary Wright: prices elevated until 'meaningful ship traffic' returns. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / IEA April OMR / CNN / Trump Truth Social / CENTCOM.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +44.3% since Feb 27 — WTI $95.94/bbl (Apr 14 close) — DOWN 3.2% — oil retreats on diplomatic hopes — gas falls to $4.118 — blockade's first full day: only 3 Hormuz transits
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 14 close: $95.94/bbl (-3.2% from Apr 13's $100.00). Oil fell on diplomatic optimism as both sides signaled willingness to return to Islamabad for Round 2. AAA national average gas: $4.118/gal (down 4 cents from Apr 13). California leads at $5.89; Oklahoma lowest at $3.27. Blockade's first full day: only 3 Hormuz transits; 600-800 vessels remain stranded (325 tankers, ~147 containerships/~470K TEU); ~20,000 seafarers trapped. IEA April OMR confirmed largest oil supply disruption in history (10.1 mb/d March loss). March CPI 3.3% YoY / +0.9% MoM released Apr 10; March PPI printed well below expectations (+0.5% vs 1.1% est.; core +0.1% vs 0.5%) easing stagflation fears. Markets now price almost no chance of a rate cut at Apr 28-29 FOMC. Source: NYMEX / AAA / BLS / Bloomberg / IEA April OMR.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.138 / +38.2% since Feb 27 — $4.118 nationally (AAA Apr 14) — DOWN 4 cents from Apr 13 — California $5.89 — EIA forecasts peak ~$4.30 in April
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Current: $4.118/gal nationally (AAA Apr 14) — down 4 cents from Apr 13 on diplomatic optimism. Most expensive state: California at $5.89; cheapest: Oklahoma at $3.27. Gas crossed $4.00/gallon Apr 2 for first time since Aug 2022. EIA forecasts monthly average peaking ~$4.30 in April. March CPI (released Apr 10) confirmed gasoline prices surged 21.2% in a single month — the largest monthly spike since 1967. Consumer cumulative fuel cost through Apr 1: $14.5B (AEI). Extra consumer fuel cost ~$40/month per driver. Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf posted Washington gas station prices, taunting: 'Enjoy the current pump figures... Soon you'll be nostalgic for $4-$5 gas.' Source: AAA / BLS / EIA / AEI.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: +38 pts / +0.6% from pre-war — S&P ~6,917 (Apr 14) — ERASES ALL WAR LOSSES — VIX drops to 18.4 lowest since war began — Nasdaq 10th consecutive up day
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Apr 14 close: ~6,917 (+0.4%) — ERASES ALL LOSSES since the war began. Nasdaq up ~0.7-1.0%, logging its 10th consecutive up day. Dow ~48,300 (+0.2%); Russell 2000 ~2,710 (+1.5%). VIX drops to ~18.4 (-3.9%) — lowest since the war began (peak was 31.05 on Mar 27). 10Y Treasury 4.29-4.30% (-0.02); DXY ~97.9-98.0 (-0.3%) near 1.25-month low; Gold ~$4,748-4,761 (+0.3%), down ~10% since war began. Markets pricing in resolution — Wall Street betting ceasefire holds and talks resume. Bank earnings drove optimism — Citigroup and Goldman Sachs beat Q1 estimates. March PPI well below expectations (+0.5% vs 1.1% est.; core +0.1% vs 0.5%) easing stagflation fears. UMich consumer sentiment hit record low in April — below both Great Recession and COVID lows. Fed holds 3.50-3.75% with markets pricing almost no chance of cut at Apr 28-29 FOMC. Source: NYSE / Bloomberg / CNBC / BLS / Federal Reserve.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$800M/day — BLOCKADE FIRST FULL DAY — only 3 Hormuz transits — Ford BREAKS post-Vietnam deployment record at 294+ days — Bush CSG entering Mediterranean
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 46: ~$800M/day — burn rate moderated as active strike operations paused during ceasefire (now ~168 hours). Daily rate likely decreased from the $1.88B/day opening pace as operations shifted from active strikes to blockade enforcement. Pentagon/Harvard/CSIS consensus current rate: $1-2B/day inclusive of all operational costs; Harvard Prof. Bilmes: $2B/day including full footprint. THAAD interceptors cost $12.7M each versus Iran's Shahed drones at $35,000 each — a 363:1 ratio. More Patriot missiles were fired in first 4 days of Iran war than the US gave Ukraine in four years. Pre-war THAAD inventory was ~534 units with no new deliveries until April 2027; at wartime usage rates, entire stockpile would be exhausted in 4-5 weeks. USNI Fleet Tracker Apr 13: 291 total Battle Force ships (232 USS, 59 USNS), 105 deployed, 86 underway, three-carrier posture. Ford at 294+ days — BREAKS post-Vietnam deployment record (previous: Lincoln 2019-20). Bush CSG entering Mediterranean ~Apr 13-14. USS Pioneer and USS Chief dispatched from Singapore for mine clearance. Pentagon $200B supplemental pending (71% of Americans oppose). 60-day War Powers deadline: April 29 — 15 days. Congress returned from 2-week recess. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / AEI / Harvard / CENTCOM / USNI Fleet Tracker / Defense Priorities.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (46 Days)
total_cost.note: ~$52.55B through Day 46 — Manifest range: $35-45B (AEI-WarCosts) vs $52B site estimate — CSIS: ~$30B — Harvard: $1T long-term — $200B supplemental pending
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Internal site tally: ~$52.55B (Day 46). External estimates vary widely: Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS (Cancian & Park): $16.5B through Day 12. AEI (McCusker) through Day 35: $22.3-31B. AEI (Pielke) / WarCosts.org through ~Day 40: ~$35B. Harvard Prof. Bilmes: $2B/day current rate, $1 trillion long-term including lifecycle costs. Daily rate has likely decreased from $1.88B/day opening pace as operations shifted from active strikes to blockade enforcement during ceasefire. Cost asymmetry: THAAD:Shahed interceptor cost ratio 363:1. Pre-war THAAD inventory (~534 units) depleting rapidly. Pentagon's $200B supplemental (quadrupled from initial $50B) has not yet been formally submitted; 71% of Americans oppose the spending. Cost to Iran (Iran govt estimate): ~$270B in damages. FDD: blockade costs Iran ~$435M/day. Consumer cumulative fuel cost through Apr 1: $14.5B (AEI). Pew Research (Mar 16-22): 61% disapprove Iran handling; only 37% approve. 29% say war worth toll (vs 59% Iraq 2003). Rep. Boebert: 'a no on any war supplemental.' Source: Pentagon / CSIS / AEI / Harvard / WarCosts.org / FDD / Pew / CNN.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CEASEFIRE ON IRAN FRONT HOLDS ~168 HRS — 35,500+ cumulative — no new strikes on Iranian territory Apr 13-14 — Battle of Bint Jbeil Day 5-6: IDF 98th Division within 300-400m of stadium — 100+ Hezbollah killed
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM Adm. Brad Cooper: 13,000+ strikes destroyed 'the military Iran built for over 40 years' — 155+ warships/submarines (largest navy elimination since WWII), 80% air defenses, 450 ballistic missile storage, 800 drone storage, 90% weapons factories, every submarine, 97% naval mines. Cumulative: 900+ strikes in first 12 hours (Feb 28); 1,879+ US-Israeli strikes documented by ACLED through mid-March across 29 of 31 provinces. No confirmed US/Israeli strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~168 hours, ceasefire technically holds). Trump: ceasefire 'holding well,' 'their military is destroyed, their whole navy is under water.' WSJ reports Trump and advisers are considering resuming limited military strikes on Iran as leverage — NOT implemented. WSJ also reports 60%+ of IRGC's naval fleet tasked with Hormuz patrol remains intact — contradicts White House claims of 97% mine elimination. On Lebanon front: Battle of Bint Jbeil Day 5-6 — IDF 98th Division within 300-400m of the stadium (site of Nasrallah's 2000 'spider's web' speech) — 100+ Hezbollah fighters killed, dozens remain active; IDF raided hospital where armed Hezbollah were sheltering. Israel maintains Lebanon never part of ceasefire (VP Vance: 'we never made that promise'). ~50,000+ US service members participated. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Pentagon / USNI / WSJ / ACLED.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 hostile KIA + 1 non-combat death — 381 WIA (CENTCOM) — Intercept disputes: 15+ KIA, 520+ injuries — no new casualties since Apr 7 ceasefire (~168 hrs) — blockade first full day saw no direct test
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. CENTCOM official: 13 killed in hostile action plus 1 non-combat death, 381 wounded (unchanged since Apr 8). Breakdown: 7 by direct enemy fire, 6 in KC-46/KC-135 crash in Iraq Mar 13, 1 health-related in Kuwait. The Intercept disputes: at least 15 KIA and 520+ total injuries including USS Ford fire casualties excluded from official counts; DCAS internal data reportedly exceeds CENTCOM's public figures by 23%. Named KIA: Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, SFC Noah Tietjens, Capt. Cody Khork. F-15E crew (494th Fighter Squadron, callsign 'Dude 44B') fully rescued Apr 4-5 from Zagros Mountains after 36-hour evasion. No new US casualties since ceasefire (8 PM ET Apr 7, ~168 hours). Combat losses include 1 F-15 (Apr 3, crew rescued), 1 A-10, multiple C-130s destroyed on ground; $1.4B+ combat losses/infrastructure damage in first 6 days (CSIS). Blockade's first full day (Apr 14) saw only 3 commercial Hormuz transits — none to Iranian ports — blockade NOT directly tested. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / The Intercept / CSIS.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 381 WIA (CENTCOM) — Intercept disputes: 520+ total injuries including USS Ford fire sailors excluded — DCAS internal data reportedly 23% above CENTCOM public figures — most injuries TBI — ceasefire ~168 hrs
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. CENTCOM official: 381 wounded (unchanged since Apr 8) — 330 returned to duty, 5 seriously wounded. The Intercept disputes: at least 520+ total injuries including 200+ USS Ford fire sailors excluded from official counts; DCAS internal data reportedly exceeds CENTCOM's public figures by 23%. Most injuries are traumatic brain injuries from blast overpressure. Ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~168 hours). No confirmed new WIA since ceasefire. Blockade's first full day (Apr 14) saw no direct engagement — only 3 Hormuz transits, none to Iranian ports. IRGC warning: any military vessel approaching Strait triggers 'harsh and decisive' force — risk remains elevated. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / The Intercept.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Health Min: 2,076+ / HRANA: 3,636 (1,701 civilians, 1,221 military, 254+ children, 240+ women) / Hengaw: 5,300+ military (Mar 18) / IDF: 6,000+ IRGC / Gap nearly 4x — no new deaths since Apr 7 ceasefire (~168 hrs)
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — deeply contested across sources with a nearly 4x spread. Iran Health Ministry (official): 2,076+ killed / 26,500+ wounded — widely seen as underestimate per IHRNGO methodology analysis. HRANA (US-based NGO): 3,636 total (1,701 civilians, 1,221 military, 714 unclassified, 254+ children, 240+ women). Hengaw (Norway-based) Mar 18: 5,300+ military killed — highest but least independently verified. IDF claims 6,000+ IRGC killed. Combined extrapolated estimate: ~9,100+. Iran's internet blackout severely limits independent verification; ACLED documented 3,000+ distinct conflict events across 29 of 31 provinces through mid-March. No Iranian deaths reported since ceasefire took effect Apr 7 (~168 hours). OCHA Apr 3: 115,193 civilian units damaged, 763 schools, 334 health/emergency centers. WHO verified 23 attacks on healthcare in Iran causing 9 deaths. Pasteur Institute (Iran's premier public health facility) severely damaged. Iran executed at least 14 protesters between Mar 17 and Apr 6 including teen wrestler Saleh Mohammadi and 18-year-old Amir Hossein Hatami (Hengaw). UNHCR $80M appeal for 2.8M affected people in Iran. Source: Iran Health Ministry / HRANA / Hengaw / IDF / OCHA / WHO / UNHCR / IHRNGO / ACLED.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 2,089+ killed (+34 from Apr 13, +69 from Apr 11), 6,762+ wounded — 1M+ displaced — Battle of Bint Jbeil Day 5-6: IDF 98th Division within 300-400m of stadium — 100+ Hezbollah killed — talks Apr 15
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli operations since March 2. Cumulative Apr 14: 2,089+ killed (+34 from Apr 13, +69 from Apr 11 baseline of 2,020), 6,762+ wounded (+174 from Apr 13, +326 from Apr 11). 252 women + 166 children + 88 medical workers killed. Displacement: 1M+ (~20% of Lebanon's population); 200,000+ fled to Syria. UNICEF: 600+ children killed or injured since March 2. Apr 8 'Black Wednesday' single-day toll documented at 357 killed (50 jets, ~160 bombs, 100 targets in 10 minutes). IDF forced evacuation orders cover ~14% of Lebanon's territory (1,470 sq km). Battle of Bint Jbeil Day 5-6: IDF 98th Division within 300-400m of the stadium — site of Nasrallah's 2000 'spider's web' speech — 100+ Hezbollah fighters killed, dozens remain active; IDF raided hospital where armed Hezbollah operatives were sheltering. Apr 13: 2 IDF soldiers moderately wounded + 6 lightly wounded by Hezbollah drone in southern Lebanon; woman injured Nahariya by Hezbollah rocket; ICRC center struck in Tyre (one killed); Lebanese Red Cross paramedic killed by Israeli drone (NBC News). Katz announced Operation 'Silver Plough' to demolish border homes. Israeli forces rammed UNIFIL vehicles with a Merkava tank on two occasions. Nine Litani River bridges destroyed; sole lifeline Qasmieh Bridge serves 71,000+. WHO: 77 attacks on healthcare, 53 health worker deaths. Food prices: vegetables +20%, bread +17% MoM. Lebanon-Israel talks Apr 15 at State Department — Israel rules out ceasefire; Hezbollah SecGen Qassem rejects as 'pointless' and 'a free concession.' Source: Lebanon Public Health EOC / CGTN / IDF / UNICEF / NBC News / WHO / UNHCR.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 7.8M+ — Iran 3.2M+ IDPs (compounded by flooding in 24 provinces) — Lebanon 1M+ (200K+ in Syria) — 55,500 Afghan refugees returned — WHO Dubai hub ON HOLD — UNHCR $80M appeal for Iran
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. Iran: 3.2 million+ internally displaced (compounded by severe flooding in 24 provinces Mar 25-30, with additional flooding Apr 5 in Rudbar/Gilan). 55,500 Afghan refugees returned to Afghanistan from Iran. 4.4M Afghans in Iran particularly vulnerable. Lebanon: 1,000,000+ displaced (~20% of population); 200,000+ fled to Syria. Humanitarian access is collapsing. WHO's Dubai logistics hub — the global health emergency hub — is ON HOLD due to regional insecurity, delaying 50+ emergency supply requests from 25 countries. Strait of Hormuz closure disrupted 80%+ of GCC food imports. UNHCR has issued an $80 million appeal for 2.8 million affected people in Iran. Iran continued executing protesters during the war — at least 14 confirmed secret executions Mar 17-Apr 6 (Hengaw). Ceasefire on Iran front holds ~168 hours but Lebanon excluded — continued Israeli operations at Bint Jbeil. Source: UNHCR / WHO / OCHA / UNICEF / IFRC / Hengaw.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — BLOCKADE FIRST FULL DAY: only 3 Hormuz transits (Rich Starry, Murlikishan, Peace Gulf — none to Iranian ports) — 600-800 vessels stranded — IEA: 10.1 mb/d March loss, largest in history
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. Hormuz blockade's first full day (Apr 14): only 3 commercial vessels transited — Rich Starry (Chinese-owned, Malawi-flagged, US-sanctioned, 250K barrels methanol; initially turned back at Qeshm Island, then passed overnight), Murlikishan (US-sanctioned, has carried Russian/Iranian oil), Peace Gulf (Panama-flagged, heading to UAE via IRGC-ordered route). None to Iranian ports — blockade NOT directly tested. Pre-war ~140 ships/day. 600-800 vessels remain stranded (325 tankers, ~147 containerships/~470K TEU); ~20,000 seafarers trapped. Dark vessel activity elevated: 3 non-AIS tankers at Iranian ports, 14 non-AIS in waiting areas. IEA April OMR confirmed 10.1 mb/d March supply loss to 97 mb/d — largest disruption in history of the global oil market; Hormuz throughput 3.8 mb/d (80%+ decline). IEA Birol: 'Worse than 1973, 1979, and 2022 combined.' CENTCOM established new routing; USS Pioneer and USS Chief dispatched from Singapore for mine clearance. IRGC previously charged tolls up to $2M per ship. Blockade costs Iran ~$435M/day (FDD). Source: ADNOC / Lloyd's List / IEA / Bloomberg / IMO / IATA / CENTCOM / Windward Maritime AI / FDD.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: HRANA Iran: 254+ children + 240+ women — UNICEF Lebanon: 600+ killed or injured since March 2 — 166 Lebanese children killed — Pope Leo XIV touring Africa on 11-day trip
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. HRANA: 254+ children killed in Iran + 240+ women. UNICEF reports 600+ children killed or injured in Lebanon since March 2; 166 Lebanese children killed total. Apr 8 'Black Wednesday' toll: 357 killed including 33 children. Key atrocities: Minab school attack (Feb 28, ~175 killed including schoolchildren) — HRW called it a war crime; Lamerd sports hall (4 children); Karaj bridge (8 civilians). 763 schools damaged, 65+ destroyed. 30+ universities struck. Pope Leo XIV led a 10,000-person Prayer Vigil for Peace at St. Peter's Basilica Apr 11, warning of the 'delusion of omnipotence' fueling war. Apr 13: Pope departed Rome for 11-day Africa trip (Algeria first), pushed back on Trump attacks from papal plane: 'I will not enter into debate... I have no fear of the Trump administration nor of speaking out loudly about the message of the Gospel. Blessed are the peacemakers.' Iran executions during war include teen wrestler Saleh Mohammadi. Source: UNICEF / HRANA / Lebanese MoH / WHO / HRW / Amnesty International / Vatican / Hengaw.
